title: Twix-style traybake
subtitle: Shortbread base with condensed milk caramel and dark chocolate
excerpt: Caramel shortbread is a three-layer traybake: a buttery baked shortbread base, a soft condensed milk caramel cooked on the hob, and a dark chocolate topping poured over when both layers are cold. Each layer must be fully set before the next goes on.
sourceNotes: Caramel shortbread, also called millionaire's shortbread, is a British and Scottish bakery staple documented in British confectionery and baking texts from the mid-twentieth century. The condensed milk caramel technique derives from Scottish tablet-making tradition. This recipe is synthesised from the established British traybake tradition.

recipe.freezeNotes: Freeze uncut in the tin before the chocolate layer if storing for longer than a week. Add the chocolate layer after thawing.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: Keeps well in an airtight container for up to five days. Best cut after the chocolate has fully set.

baking.bakeTemperatureNote: fan or conventional; low heat gives the shortbread an even pale golden colour without darkening the edges

glossaryTerms.definition: Condensed milk caramel is made by simmering condensed milk with butter and golden syrup over medium heat, stirring continuously, until the mixture deepens to a warm amber and thickens enough to pull away from the sides of the pan. This takes 8 to 12 minutes. Do not leave the pan; the sugars catch easily on the base. The finished caramel holds a soft ribbon when dripped from the spoon and sets to a fudge-like layer once cool. If the caramel is pale and runny, cook it for longer.

content.text: The key to this traybake is patience between layers. Make the shortbread first, then the
while it cools, then the chocolate once the caramel is cold and firm. Rushing any stage results in layers that slide apart.

Heat the oven to 160 degrees C. Line a 20 cm square tin with baking paper. Beat the 100 g softened butter and 50 g icing sugar together until pale. Add the 200 g flour and mix to a soft dough. Press evenly into the tin with your knuckles. Prick all over with a fork. Bake for 22 to 25 minutes until pale golden and dry to the touch. Leave to cool completely in the tin.

Put the 50 g butter, 397 g condensed milk, 3 tbsp golden syrup and quarter teaspoon salt into a medium saucepan. Stir over medium heat until the butter melts. Bring to a steady simmer, then cook for 8 to 12 minutes, stirring continuously, until the caramel thickens, deepens to amber, and pulls away from the sides of the pan. Pour over the cooled shortbread and spread level. Leave until fully cold, about 30 minutes at room temperature.

Melt the 200 g dark chocolate in a heatproof bowl set over a pan of barely simmering water, or in short bursts in the microwave. Pour over the cold caramel and spread to the edges. Refrigerate for 30 minutes until the chocolate is fully set. Lift out of the tin using the baking paper. Score the chocolate with a hot knife before cutting to prevent cracking, then cut into 16 bars.

items.solution: The caramel was cooked at too high a heat, causing the sugar to crystallise. Next time use medium heat and stir continuously from the start. If graininess appears while cooking, add a teaspoon of cold water and stir off the heat briefly before continuing.
The chocolate was too cold and fully set hard. Run a sharp knife under very hot water and dry it before each cut. Scoring a line through the chocolate before it fully sets also helps.
The caramel was poured onto a warm shortbread or the chocolate was added before the caramel cooled. Each layer must be fully cold before the next is added.
